source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/Bulletin17CAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- A11 EXACT oracle, verified against the real RMC.BestFit Bulletin17CAnalysis by the dotnet gate. Bulletin17CAnalysis fits an LP3 model by GMM (default MultivariateNormal UQ) then computes the deterministic Cohn-style delta-method CIs (nested Gaussian quadrature over the GMM fit -- no MCMC/bootstrap seed dependence given the fitted parameters). The GMM fit uses BFGS + a numerical Jacobian (no RNG), so the parameters/point_estimates/CIs reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python; parameter + point_estimate match C# to rel 1e-9, the Cohn CI bounds to rel 1e-8 (the nested quadrature is a distinct summation path). confidence_level and the exceedance-probability round-trip are exact structural invariants. The 25 default exceedance ordinates ascend, so the point-estimate curve descends.
